title: A Deep-sworn Vow
content:
Others because you did not keep
That deep-sworn vow have been friends of mine;
Yet always when I look death in the face,
When I clamber to the heights of sleep,
Or when I grow excited with wine,
Suddenly I meet your face.
meaning:
William Butler Yeats's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Deep-sworn Vow"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
